blockingIssues.description: CRITICAL: The kb-compile feature implementation is MISSING ENTIRELY (Round 6 confirmation). The compileCommand() function is referenced in registerKbCommands() (line545: 'await compileCommand()') but NO FUNCTION DEFINITION EXISTS anywhere in the codebase. This feature was marked 'completed' in features.json but NO implementation code was ever written.
evidence: Grep for 'async function compileCommand|export async function compileCommand|function compileCommand' finds NO matches (verified Round 6)
kb.ts file shows: ingestCommand (defined line67), normalizeCommand (defined line 254), outputCommand (defined lines 311and320 - duplicate), helpCommand (defined line 165)
kb.ts line 545: 'await compileCommand()' is called but function undefined
Working tree is clean (git status shows nothing to commit)
Latest commit: 'validation(scrutiny): wiki-compile milestone round 5 re-validation confirms blocking issue persists'
wiki/ directory contains only .gitkeep - no wiki pages were ever generated
Running 'pnpm paperclipai kb:compile' would throw runtime error: compileCommand is not defined
SAME blocking issue documented in Round1, Round 2, Round 3, Round 4, Round 5, and now Round 6 - NO FIX COMMITTED
blockingIssues.suggestedFix: Implement the compileCommand function in cli/src/commands/kb.ts to: (1) Read all normalized .md files from company-hq/kb/normalized/, (2) Create company-hq/kb/wiki/index.md with conversation list (titles, dates, UUIDs), (3) Create individual topic pages in wiki/ directory, (4) Include source references in frontmatter (source conversation UUID, dates)

blockingIssues.description: Milestone assertions VAL-COMPILE-001 through VAL-COMPILE-004 CANNOT BE VALIDATED. These assertions require: index.md creation (VAL-COMPILE-001), topic pages creation (VAL-COMPILE-002), source references in frontmatter (VAL-COMPILE-003), and successful compile execution (VAL-COMPILE-004). None of these can pass when the command function doesn't exist.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add explicit implementation verification step in kb-worker skill: Before marking a feature as 'completed', the worker must verify the implementation exists by (1) grepping for the function definition, (2) running the CLI command to confirm it doesn't throw 'function not defined', and (3) checking that actual output files are created.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The kb-compile feature was marked 'completed' for 6 validation rounds withoutany implementation code existing. The compileCommand() function was referenced but never defined.

conclusion: Round 6 scrutiny validation CONFIRMS: The kb-compile feature implementation is MISSING ENTIRELY. This is the SIXTH consecutive round with the SAME critical blocking issue. The compileCommand() function is referenced in the CLI command registration (registerKbCommands) but NEVER implemented. The kb.ts file shows: ingestCommand (defined), normalizeCommand (defined), outputCommand (defined twice), helpCommand (defined), but compileCommand is CALLED but NEVER DEFINED. Feature reviews were NOT spawned because there is nothing to review - the function does not exist. This requires URGENT orchestrator attention: either create a new fix feature with explicit implementation requirements, or assign to a worker who will actually implement compileCommand(). The feature has been incorrectly marked 'completed' for 6 validation rounds without any implementation code being written.
